name: Nine-orbit three-class hard-tail discriminator
hypothesis: The strengthened nine-orbit blocker separates at least one structural hard-tail class at the unchanged 60-second cap.
expected_signal: Exactly three outcomes for s-r0-1, s-r1-15, and t-10; replayable UNSAT, validated SAT orbit, or fixed-cap timeout.
decision_value: Distinguishes root-0 secondary, root-1 secondary, and tertiary hard-tail behavior before any broader allocation.

efficiency_design.naive_cost: A generic pass over all 12 hard-tail nodes would spend up to 720 solver-seconds before review.
efficiency_design.chosen_reductions: Exactly three structurally distinct representatives at the unchanged 60-second cap.
efficiency_design.expected_throughput_gain: Measures blocker sensitivity and class behavior using 25% of a full hard-tail sweep.
efficiency_design.soundness_basis: Hash-bound nine-orbit blocker/frontier and complete 32-certificate disjointness gate; independent audit required for every decisive result.
efficiency_design.remains_uncompressed: Nine-node hard tail and catalogue exhaustion remain after this discriminator.
